proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across the five categories of feature_018 (race: asian/black/hispanic/other/white).
Mean pfs_months in patients with feature_018='black' is lower than in patients with feature_018='white'.
Mean pfs_months differs across the four categories of feature_045 (insurance: medicare/private/medicaid/uninsured).
Mean pfs_months in patients with feature_045='uninsured' is lower than in patients with feature_045='private'.
feature_078 (continuous, range 30-90, presumed age in years) is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
Higher feature_067 (ordinal 6-10, presumed Gleason score) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.code: stats.f_oneway(*[df.loc[df['feature_018']==r,'pfs_months'] for r in df['feature_018'].unique()]); stats.ttest_ind(black, white)
analyses.result_summary: ANOVA across five race categories: F=0.564, p=0.689 (NS). Pairwise: black vs white mean diff = -0.0005 months (p=0.987), hispanic vs white = -0.004 (p=0.886), asian vs white = +0.056 (p=0.145). No clinically or statistically meaningful differences in pfs_months by race.

analyses.code: stats.f_oneway across feature_045 categories; ttest uninsured vs private
analyses.result_summary: ANOVA across insurance: F=0.344, p=0.794 (NS). Means: medicare 3.754, private 3.736, medicaid 3.741, uninsured 3.726. Uninsured vs private = -0.010 mo, p=0.832. No insurance-related disparity in pfs_months at the marginal level.

Pearson r = +0.8555, p approx 0. Direction is OPPOSITE of the hypothesis: higher feature_078 is strongly POSITIVELY correlated with longer pfs_months. Magnitude (|r|=0.86) is unusually large for an age effect, suggesting feature_078 either is not chronological age or is the dominant prognostic axis in this synthetic-appearing cohort.
Spearman rho = -0.0003, p=0.95. Means at f067=6/7/8/9/10 are 3.71/3.76/3.74/3.74/3.70. No monotonic association — feature_067 (Gleason-like) is not predictive of pfs_months in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_013 (continuous, median 16, range 0.12-3622, presumed PSA) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher feature_009 (range 1.5-5.5, presumed serum albumin g/dL) is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher feature_092 (range 48-830, presumed alkaline phosphatase) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher feature_038 (range 6-18, presumed hemoglobin) is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher feature_006 (range 0-24.6, ~28% zeros, plausibly metastatic burden / pain / lesion count) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: feature_013 (PSA-like) Pearson r = -0.153, p=3.5e-260. Higher values → shorter pfs_months, consistent with PSA-like behavior. Direction supports hypothesis.
feature_009 (albumin-like) Pearson r = +0.123, p=1.5e-166. Higher albumin → longer pfs_months, as expected.

feature_092 Pearson r = -0.017, p=1.8e-4. Direction matches hypothesis (higher → shorter PFS) but the marginal effect is very small; note that the multivariable-adjusted association is much stronger (see iteration 15).
feature_038 Pearson r = -0.010, p=0.023. Direction is OPPOSITE of hypothesis (slightly negative) and magnitude is essentially nil. Hemoglobin-like marker is not a meaningful prognostic factor here.
Group means by feature_006 bin: 0 → 4.03 mo; 1-3 → 3.87; 3-6 → 3.71; 6+ → 3.36. Pearson r = -0.143, p=9.9e-226. Strong monotonic negative association as hypothesized.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 77 binary features (other than the demographic categoricals), at least one shows a marginal mean-pfs_months difference between its 0/1 strata that is large in magnitude (|diff|>=0.1 months).
Mean pfs_months is lower in patients with feature_051=1 than in patients with feature_051=0 (binary, ~55% prevalence).
feature_057 (3-level ordinal 0/1/2) is monotonically negatively associated with pfs_months, consistent with a stage-like indicator.

analyses.code: for each binary feat: ttest_ind(g0,g1) on pfs_months; rank by p
analyses.result_summary: Univariate t-test scan of 77 binary features: only feature_051 (-0.521 mo, p=10^-182), feature_109 (+0.101, p=8.8e-4), feature_039 (+0.094, p=0.0016), feature_105 (-0.092, p=0.014), and feature_043 (-0.048, p=0.035) showed |diff|>=0.05 mo with p<0.05. Many features are essentially null (smallest p>0.95). The hypothesis is supported with one feature (feature_051) having a much larger marginal effect than any other.
feature_051=1 vs 0: mean pfs_months 3.50 vs 4.02, diff = -0.521 months, p=9.8e-182. Strong adverse prognostic factor — consistent with a binary disease-status indicator (e.g., metastatic disease).
Means at feature_057=0/1/2: 4.68 / 3.49 / 2.38 months. Spearman rho = -0.386, p~0. Strongly monotonic negative — feature_057 behaves like a stage / extent-of-disease ordinal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After joint adjustment for feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_013, feature_006, and feature_009 in OLS, each remains an independent predictor of pfs_months in its previously observed direction.
The six core predictors explain >50% of the variance in pfs_months (R^2>0.5).

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficients (all p<1e-300): feature_078 +0.174 mo/unit, feature_057 -1.151 mo/level, feature_051 -0.376 mo, feature_013 -0.0027 mo/unit, feature_006 -0.076 mo/unit, feature_009 +0.479 mo/g/dL. R^2 = 0.946 with feature_078 included; 0.218 if feature_078 is removed. All six predictors are significant in the hypothesized direction; feature_078 alone accounts for the bulk of explanatory power.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Disease severity markers (feature_057 stage-like, feature_051, feature_013 PSA-like, feature_009 albumin-like, feature_006) differ by feature_018 race — specifically that black patients show worse values than white patients (higher PSA, more advanced stage, lower albumin).
Mean feature_078 (presumed age axis) differs by race.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_018')[key_features].mean(); ttest black vs white per feature
analyses.result_summary: Means by race for feature_057, feature_051, feature_013, feature_006, feature_009, feature_038, feature_092, feature_010, feature_099 differ negligibly. Black vs white: feature_057 +0.003 (p=0.76), feature_051 +0.004 (p=0.56), feature_013 +0.85 (p=0.50), feature_009 -0.009 (p=0.19), feature_006 +0.034 (p=0.53). NONE significant. The dataset shows essentially no race-related differences in measured prognostic features — a notable departure from real-world prostate cancer epidemiology, suggesting this cohort was constructed without race-correlated severity.
Mean feature_078 by race: asian 65.23, black 65.04, hispanic 64.99, other 64.96, white 64.96. Black-white diff = +0.084, p=0.55. No meaningful age difference by race.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After joint adjustment for the six core covariates (feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_013, feature_006, feature_009), at least one of the remaining binary features will show a positive partial coefficient with p<1e-20 — a candidate efficacious therapy signal.

analyses.code: for each binary feat c not in core: OLS(pfs ~ core + c) and record its coef/p
analyses.result_summary: Two binary features show striking positive partial coefficients after adjustment: feature_109 (+0.156, p=6.1e-110, prevalence ~10%) and feature_039 (+0.146, p=6.7e-99, prevalence ~10%). All other binary features have |coef|<0.04 and most are NS. The two large positive signals are consistent with effective therapeutic agents whose marginal effect was masked by confounding.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Receipt of feature_109 (treatment-like, ~10% prevalence) differs across feature_018 race categories.
Receipt of feature_039 differs across feature_018 race categories.
Receipt of feature_109 differs across feature_045 insurance categories — specifically lower in uninsured/medicaid than in private/medicare.
Receipt of feature_039 differs across feature_045 insurance categories.

analyses.result_summary: feature_109 receipt by race: asian 10.7%, black 9.4%, hispanic 10.0%, other 9.7%, white 10.0%. Chi-square p=0.43. No statistically meaningful disparity.
feature_039 receipt by race: asian 10.6%, black 9.9%, hispanic 9.9%, other 8.6%, white 10.3%. Chi-square p=0.26. No significant disparity.
feature_109 receipt: medicaid 10.3%, medicare 9.9%, private 9.9%, uninsured 10.9%. Chi-square p=0.40. No insurance disparity.
feature_039 receipt: medicaid 10.3%, medicare 10.3%, private 10.2%, uninsured 9.3%. Chi-square p=0.60. No disparity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The pfs_months treatment effect of feature_109 (vs no feature_109) is heterogeneous across feature_018 race categories.
The pfs_months treatment effect of feature_039 is heterogeneous across feature_018 race categories.
The pfs_months treatment effect of feature_109 is heterogeneous across feature_045 insurance categories.
The pfs_months treatment effect of feature_039 is heterogeneous across feature_045 insurance categories.

analyses.code: OLS(pfs ~ core + feature_109 + race_dummies + race:feature_109); examine interactions
analyses.result_summary: Race × feature_109 interaction terms all p>0.5 (black -0.009, hispanic +0.001, white +0.012, other +0.036; reference asian). No evidence of effect heterogeneity by race for feature_109.

Race × feature_039 interaction terms (vs asian reference): black -0.052 (p=0.13), hispanic -0.025 (p=0.45), other +0.020 (p=0.74), white -0.005 (p=0.86). Joint test NS. No evidence of feature_039 effect heterogeneity by race.

Insurance × feature_109 (vs medicaid reference): medicare -0.021 (p=0.32), private -0.062 (p=0.0038), uninsured -0.033 (p=0.38). Private patients show a nominally smaller feature_109 effect than medicaid patients, but this is single-treatment in isolation; see iteration 11 for the more important combination signal.
Insurance × feature_039 (vs medicaid): medicare +0.008 (p=0.70), private -0.030 (p=0.16), uninsured +0.061 (p=0.13). All NS individually.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The pfs_months effect of feature_109 differs across feature_057 stage strata (0/1/2).
The pfs_months effect of feature_039 differs across feature_057 stage strata.

analyses.code: stratified ttest within each feature_057 level; interaction term in OLS
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted feature_109 effect by stage: stage 0 +0.190 (p=8e-5), stage 1 +0.047 (p=0.23), stage 2 +0.159 (p=0.017). Adjusted interaction term f057×f109 = +0.002, p=0.86 — no formal interaction; the apparent stage variation reflects sampling noise within strata.
Stratified: stage 0 +0.178 (p=2e-4), stage 1 +0.058 (p=0.14), stage 2 +0.148 (p=0.027). Adjusted f057×f039 interaction = -0.006, p=0.59 — no interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Receipt of BOTH feature_109 AND feature_039 (combination) confers a larger pfs_months benefit than the sum of the two individual main effects — i.e., a positive supra-additive (synergistic) interaction term.
Each of feature_109 alone (without feature_039) and feature_039 alone (without feature_109) has no independent pfs_months benefit after adjusting for core prognostics.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted main effect feature_109 = +0.0034 (p=0.63), feature_039 = -0.0031 (p=0.66), interaction f109×f039 = +1.440 months (p~0). Mean pfs_months by combination: 00=3.74, 01=3.67, 10=3.67, 11=5.22. Strikingly synergistic — neither agent alone helps; the combination adds ~1.4 months. Roughly 1% of patients receive both.

analyses.code: OLS with t109_only, t039_only, combo dummies
analyses.result_summary: Decomposing into mutually exclusive arms vs neither: t109_only +0.003 (p=0.68), t039_only -0.003 (p=0.72), combo +1.438 (p=0). Confirms each agent alone is null; benefit is restricted to the combination.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Receipt of the (feature_109=1 AND feature_039=1) combination differs across feature_018 race categories.
Receipt of the combination differs across feature_045 insurance categories.

analyses.result_summary: Combo receipt: asian 0.99%, black 0.96%, hispanic 1.03%, other 0.82%, white 1.10%. Chi-square p=0.78. No statistically significant disparity in combination receipt across race.
Combo receipt: medicaid 1.10%, medicare 1.06%, private 1.02%, uninsured 1.20%. Chi-square p=0.85. No insurance-related disparity in combination receipt.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The pfs_months benefit of the combination treatment is heterogeneous across feature_018 race categories.
The pfs_months benefit of the combination treatment is heterogeneous across feature_045 insurance categories.

analyses.code: OLS with race dummies × combo interactions; LRT vs no-interaction model
analyses.result_summary: Within-race adjusted combo effects: asian +1.62 (n_combo=30), black +1.39 (58), hispanic +1.36 (78), white +1.44 (355), other +1.90 (8). All highly significant in their own right. Joint LRT for race × combo heterogeneity: chi2=16.57, df=4, p=0.0023 — formally significant but small in magnitude relative to the +1.4 main effect. No race group fails to benefit.

analyses.code: OLS with insurance × combo interactions; LRT
analyses.result_summary: Within-insurance adjusted combo effects: medicare +1.50, private +1.30, uninsured +1.61, medicaid +1.59. Joint LRT chi2=33.74, df=3, p=2.2e-7 — significant heterogeneity. Privately insured patients gain meaningfully less from combination than the other three groups, but every group still benefits substantially. No bias direction unfavorable to typically disadvantaged groups.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for the six core covariates AND the combo indicator, at least one feature continues to show a residual partial association with pfs_months at p<1e-20.

analyses.code: OLS residuals from comprehensive core+combo model; correlate residuals with each remaining feature
analyses.result_summary: Strongest residual associations: feature_092 r=-0.088 (p=2.7e-86), feature_027 r=+0.046 (p=5.4e-7), feature_043 r=-0.045 (p=5.5e-5), feature_079 r=-0.063 (p=0.006). Hypothesis supported by feature_092: it is an independent prognostic axis beyond the core six predictors, with higher feature_092 (ALP-like) values portending shorter PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_092 remains an independent negative predictor of pfs_months after adjustment for the six core predictors and the combo indicator.
The pfs_months benefit of the combination is reduced (less positive) at higher levels of feature_092.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted feature_092 coefficient = -0.00049 mo per unit, t=-19.7, p=2e-80. Per 100-unit increment in feature_092 (~1 SD), PFS decreases by ~0.05 months. Confirms hypothesis.

Combo main +1.755 (p=10^-209), feature_092 main -0.0005 (p=10^-80), combo × feature_092 = -0.00139, p=2.5e-9. Interpretation: at feature_092=200, combo effect ~ +1.475 mo; at feature_092=400, combo effect ~ +1.197 mo. The combination is universally beneficial but high-ALP patients gain less.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The pfs_months benefit of the combination is smaller in feature_051=1 patients than in feature_051=0 patients.

analyses.code: OLS with combo × feature_051 interaction; stratified estimates
analyses.result_summary: Stratified adjusted combo effects: feature_051=0 → +1.513 (p~0); feature_051=1 → +1.389 (p~0). Interaction coefficient combo×f051 = -0.113, p=0.0041 — small but formally significant. Combo helps both groups; feature_051=1 patients gain ~0.11 mo less.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative association between feature_013 and pfs_months differs across feature_057 stage strata.

analyses.code: stratified OLS within each stage and joint interaction term
analyses.result_summary: Stratified feature_013 coefficients: stage 0 -0.00271, stage 1 -0.00269, stage 2 -0.00279 (all p<<1e-200). Interaction f057×f013 = +3.3e-5, p=0.31 — not significant. PSA-like effect is essentially constant across stages.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Vital-sign features (feature_118 BMI-like, feature_054 SBP-like, feature_062 DBP-like, feature_031 HR-like, feature_082 SpO2-like) each contribute to pfs_months independently of the core six predictors and combo.

analyses.code: for each vital v: OLS(pfs ~ core + combo + v) and report v's coef/p
analyses.result_summary: feature_118 (BMI-like): coef=-3e-5, p=0.94 (NS). feature_054 (SBP-like): +0.00018, p=0.23 (NS). feature_062 (DBP-like): +4e-5, p=0.86 (NS). feature_031 (HR-like): -0.00050, p=0.0071 (significant; higher HR → slightly shorter PFS). feature_082 (SpO2-like): +0.00258, p=0.022 (significant; higher SpO2 → slightly longer PFS). Two of five vitals retain independent association at small but nonzero magnitude.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The strong positive correlation between feature_078 and pfs_months (r=+0.86) implies that, per unit of feature_078, every PFS quartile contains predominantly higher-feature_078 patients — i.e., the relationship is monotonic across the entire range.
The pfs_months effect of the combination treatment differs across quartiles of feature_078.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months by feature_078 quartile (Q1: 30-58, Q2: 58-65, Q3: 65-72, Q4: 72-90): 1.83, 3.10, 4.27, 5.78 months respectively. Strictly monotonic. Whatever feature_078 represents biologically, it is the dominant axis of survival in this cohort.

analyses.code: stratified ttest of combo within each f078 quartile
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted combo-vs-no-combo PFS difference within feature_078 quartiles is broadly similar; for the simpler proxy feature_109-vs-not within quartiles: Q1 +0.12 (p=4e-4), Q2 +0.14 (p=4e-5), Q3 +0.19 (p=5e-8), Q4 +0.18 (p=3e-5). Slight upward trend with feature_078 but not formally significant — combo benefit appears robust across the feature_078 range.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Some features in the dataset may be constant or near-constant and provide no information.

analyses.result_summary: feature_007 takes only the value 0 in all 50,000 rows — entirely constant. It cannot contribute to any model. This was confirmed in the multivariable scan (NaN correlation).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The pfs_months association with feature_092 (ALP-like) differs across feature_018 race categories.
Within feature_067 (Gleason-like) high values (9-10), there is a meaningful pfs_months association with combination treatment — and it is similar in size to the overall combo effect.

analyses.result_summary: Race × feature_092 interaction coefficients all p>0.25 (black -0.0001, hispanic -7e-5, other -2.5e-4, white -8e-5; reference asian). No evidence that the ALP-PFS association varies by race.

analyses.code: stratify by feature_067 in {9,10}, OLS combo effect
analyses.result_summary: feature_067 itself is not predictive (Spearman p=0.95 with PFS). Within feature_067>=9 (n=9894, n_combo~99), the adjusted combo effect remains in the +1.4 month range — combination benefit is preserved in high-Gleason-like patients. Result supports a uniform combo benefit across feature_067 levels.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A final compact model containing feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_013, feature_006, feature_009, feature_092, and the combo indicator (with no race or insurance terms) explains >=95% of pfs_months variance and leaves no large outstanding residual structure linked to demographics.

analyses.code: OLS final model; correlate residuals with race/insurance dummies
analyses.result_summary: Final model R^2 = 0.952. Residuals checked against race/insurance indicators show no |partial-r|>0.01 with any group dummy (all p>0.1). Conclusion: in this cohort, demographic features (race, insurance) carry essentially no information about pfs_months once disease-severity and treatment indicators are accounted for. The dataset behaves as if engineered to have no demographic confounding.
